en: Then Elihu answered and said:
zh: 以利戶回答，說：

en: "Hear my words, you wise men, and give ear to me, you who know;
zh: “智慧人哪！你們要聽我的話； 有知識的人哪！你們要側耳聽我。

en: for the ear tests words as the palate tastes food.
zh: 因為耳朵試驗言語， 好像上膛嘗試食物。

en: Let us choose what is right; let us know among ourselves what is good.
zh: 我們要選擇何為是， 讓我們彼此知道何為善。

en: For Job has said, 'I am in the right, and God has taken away my right;
zh: 約伯說：‘我是有理的， 神卻奪去我的公理；

en: in spite of my right I am counted a liar; my wound is incurable, though I am without transgression.'
zh: 我雖然有公理，卻被認為是說謊的； 我雖然毫無過犯，我所受的箭傷卻無法醫治。’

en: What man is like Job, who drinks up scoffing like water,
zh: 有哪一個人像約伯呢？ 他喝褻慢如同喝水，

en: who travels in company with evildoers and walks with wicked men?
zh: 他與作孽的同夥， 他與惡人同行。

en: For he has said, 'It profits a man nothing that he should take delight in God.'
zh: 因為他說過：‘人討 神的喜悅， 對他並無益處。’

en: "Therefore, hear me, you men of understanding: far be it from God that he should do wickedness, and from the Almighty that he should do wrong.
zh: 所以，明理的人哪！你們要聽我的話， 神決不至作惡，全能者斷不至行不義，

en: For according to the work of a man he will repay him, and according to his ways he will make it befall him.
zh: 因為 神必照著人所作的報應他， 使各人按照所行的得報應。

en: Of a truth, God will not do wickedly, and the Almighty will not pervert justice.
zh: 真的， 神必不作惡， 全能者也必不顛倒是非。

en: Who gave him charge over the earth, and who laid on him the whole world?
zh: 誰派他管理大地？ 誰設立全世界呢？

en: If he should set his heart to it and gather to himself his spirit and his breath,
zh: 他若對人決心這樣作， 他若把靈與氣收歸自己，

en: all flesh would perish together, and man would return to dust.
zh: 有血肉生命的就都一同氣絕身亡， 世人都歸回塵土。

en: "If you have understanding, hear this; listen to what I say.
zh: 你若明理，就請聽這話， 留心聽我說話的聲音。

en: Shall one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn him who is righteous and mighty,
zh: 恨惡公平的真的可以掌權嗎？ 那有公義與大能的，你定他為有罪嗎？

en: who says to a king, 'Worthless one,' and to nobles, 'Wicked man,'
zh: 他不是對君王說：‘你是匪徒’， 又對貴族說：‘你們是惡人’嗎？

en: who shows no partiality to princes, nor regards the rich more than the poor, for they are all the work of his hands?
zh: 他不徇王子的情面， 也不看重富人過於窮人， 因為他們都是他手所造的。

en: In a moment they die; at midnight the people are shaken and pass away, and the mighty are taken away by no human hand.
zh: 他們在半夜之中剎那之間死亡， 平民因遭受震動而去世， 有權勢的被除滅非借人手。

en: "For his eyes are on the ways of a man, and he sees all his steps.
zh: 神的眼看顧人的道路， 察看他每一步，

en: There is no gloom or deep darkness where evildoers may hide themselves.
zh: 沒有黑暗，又沒有死蔭的地方， 可以給作孽的在那裡藏身。

en: For God has no need to consider a man further, that he should go before God in judgment.
zh: 神不必進一步鑒察人， 使人到他面前去受審判。

en: He shatters the mighty without investigation and sets others in their place.
zh: 他不必查究就可以打碎有權勢的人， 並且設立別人代替他們。

en: Thus, knowing their works, he overturns them in the night, and they are crushed.
zh: 他原來留心他們所行的， 在夜間傾覆他們，他們就被壓碎；

en: He strikes them for their wickedness in a place for all to see,
zh: 他在觀眾面前擊打他們， 有如擊打惡人一樣；

en: because they turned aside from following him and had no regard for any of his ways,
zh: 因為他們偏離正道不跟從他， 也不留心他的一切道路，

en: so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him, and he heard the cry of the afflicted-
zh: 以致窮人的哀求達到他那裡， 他也垂聽了困苦人的哀求。

en: When he is quiet, who can condemn? When he hides his face, who can behold him, whether it be a nation or a man?-
zh: 他若安靜不動，誰能定他有罪呢？ 他若掩面，誰能看見他呢？ 無論對待一國或一人，都是這樣。

en: that a godless man should not reign, that he should not ensnare the people.
zh: 別讓不敬虔的人作王， 免得他危害人民。

en: "For has anyone said to God, 'I have borne punishment; I will not offend any more;
zh: 有人對 神說： ‘我忍受了管教，不再有敗壞的行為了，

en: teach me what I do not see; if I have done iniquity, I will do it no more'?
zh: 我所看不到的求你指教我， 我若行了罪孽，必不再行’。

en: Will he then make repayment to suit you, because you reject it? For you must choose, and not I; therefore declare what you know.
zh: 神會因你拒絕悔改， 就遵照你的心意嗎？ 要作抉擇的是你，不是我， 你所知道的，只管說出來吧。

en: Men of understanding will say to me, and the wise man who hears me will say:
zh: 明理的人必對我說， 智慧的人要聽我的話。

en: 'Job speaks without knowledge; his words are without insight.'
zh: 約伯說話毫無知識， 他的言語沒有智慧。

en: Would that Job were tried to the end, because he answers like wicked men.
zh: 但願約伯被試驗到底， 因為他的回答像惡人一樣。

en: For he adds rebellion to his sin; he claps his hands among us and multiplies his words against God."
zh: 他在自己的罪上又加過犯， 在我們中間鼓掌嘲笑 神， 用許多話敵對 神。”
